strengths: Distributed systems and real-time performance expertise—Dunnhumby (millions of requests/sec), low-latency DLang systems, asynchronous I/O
Recent exposure to agentic AI infrastructure at re:cinq with MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
20-year career with consistent technical progression to Staff roles across web, embedded, and distributed systems
concerns: No production multi-agent AI systems demonstrated—recent re:cinq role is nascent; lacks evidence of stateful agents, feedback loops, or evaluation infrastructure
Zero fintech infrastructure experience—no background in credit systems, payment flows, risk assessment, or regulatory compliance despite being core to the role
No track record scaling teams to 30+ engineers or developing senior technical leadership—resume shows individual and small-team contributions, not C-level organizational building

key_factors.factor: No demonstrated engineering team scaling or technical leadership development
Recent agentic AI infrastructure work at re:cinq

response.justification: While Szabo brings strong distributed-systems expertise and recent agentic AI infrastructure work, he lacks two critical CTO qualifications: fintech infrastructure experience and a demonstrated track record scaling engineering teams to 30+ people. His re:cinq role is encouraging but too nascent to offset these gaps. He would be better suited for a senior AI systems architect position.
